metadata.name: RMF-IS27005 Risk Management Framework Ontology
metadata.description: Risk Management Framework ontology aligned with ISO/IEC 27005 information security risk management. Provides comprehensive risk identification, assessment, treatment, and monitoring capabilities that bridge to RCSG-Series compliance and security ontologies.
metadata.purpose: Define canonical risk management concepts for ISO 27005-aligned risk assessment workflows, enabling full risk-to-RCSG-scope management across regulatory, compliance, security and governance dimensions
metadata.creator: OAA Generator v6.1.0

entities.name: RiskContext
entities.description: The organisational and information security context establishing scope, boundaries, and criteria for risk management activities

properties.name: contextId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the risk context

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Boundaries and scope of risk management

properties.name: organisationUnit
properties.description: Organisation unit or business area in scope

properties.name: regulatoryEnvironment
properties.description: Applicable regulatory and legal requirements

properties.name: riskAppetiteStatement
properties.description: Organisational risk appetite and tolerance levels

entities.name: Asset
entities.description: An information asset, system, or resource of value to the organisation that requires protection from security threats

properties.name: assetId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the asset

properties.name: assetName
properties.description: Human-readable name for the asset

properties.name: assetType
properties.description: Classification type: information, software, physical, service, person, or intangible

properties.name: criticality
properties.description: Business criticality rating: critical, high, medium, or low

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Person or team responsible for the asset

properties.name: classificationLevel
properties.description: Data classification: public, internal, confidential, or restricted

entities.name: Threat
entities.description: A potential cause of an unwanted incident that may result in harm to an asset or the organisation as a whole

properties.name: threatId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the threat

properties.name: threatName
properties.description: Descriptive name of the threat

properties.name: threatCategory
properties.description: Category: deliberate, accidental, environmental, or structural

properties.name: threatSource
properties.description: Origin of the threat: external attacker, insider, natural event, etc.

properties.name: likelihood
properties.description: Assessed likelihood of occurrence: very-low, low, medium, high, very-high

entities.name: Vulnerability
entities.description: A weakness in an asset or control that can be exploited by one or more threats to cause harm to the organisation

properties.name: vulnerabilityId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the vulnerability

properties.name: vulnerabilityName
properties.description: Descriptive name of the vulnerability

properties.name: severity
properties.description: Severity rating: critical, high, medium, low, informational

properties.name: exploitability
properties.description: Ease of exploitation: trivial, moderate, difficult

properties.name: affectedLayer
properties.description: Technology layer affected: network, application, physical, process, or personnel

entities.name: Risk
entities.description: A potential event where a threat exploits a vulnerability to adversely affect an asset, expressed as likelihood and impact

properties.name: riskId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the risk

properties.name: riskTitle
properties.description: Short descriptive title of the risk scenario

properties.name: riskDescription
properties.description: Detailed description of the risk scenario

properties.name: inherentLikelihood
properties.description: Likelihood before controls: very-low, low, medium, high, very-high

properties.name: inherentImpact
properties.description: Impact before controls: negligible, minor, moderate, major, catastrophic

properties.name: inherentRiskLevel
properties.description: Calculated inherent risk level: low, medium, high, critical

properties.name: residualRiskLevel
properties.description: Risk level after treatment controls are applied

properties.name: riskStatus
properties.description: Current status: identified, assessed, treating, accepted, closed

entities.name: RiskAssessment
entities.description: A structured evaluation exercise that identifies, analyses, and evaluates information security risks within a defined context

properties.name: assessmentId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the risk assessment

properties.name: assessmentDate
properties.description: Date the risk assessment was conducted

properties.name: methodology
properties.description: Risk assessment methodology used (e.g., qualitative, quantitative, semi-quantitative)

properties.name: assessmentStatus
properties.description: Status of the assessment: planned, in-progress, completed, approved

properties.name: assessor
properties.description: Person or team conducting the assessment

properties.name: nextReviewDate
properties.description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review

entities.name: RiskTreatment
entities.description: A planned response to an identified risk specifying the treatment strategy and actions to reduce risk to acceptable levels

properties.name: treatmentId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the treatment plan

properties.name: treatmentStrategy
properties.description: Strategy: mitigate, accept, transfer, avoid

properties.name: treatmentDescription
properties.description: Detailed description of treatment actions

properties.name: implementationDate
properties.description: Target date for treatment implementation

properties.name: treatmentStatus
properties.description: Status: planned, in-progress, implemented, verified

properties.name: costEstimate
properties.description: Estimated cost of implementing the treatment

entities.name: Control
entities.description: A security control or safeguard implemented to reduce the likelihood or impact of a risk, aligned with ISO 27001 Annex A

properties.name: controlId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the control (e.g., ISO 27001 Annex A reference)

properties.name: controlName
properties.description: Name of the security control

properties.name: controlType
properties.description: Type: preventive, detective, corrective, deterrent, compensating

properties.name: controlCategory
properties.description: Category: technical, organisational, physical, or legal

properties.name: effectiveness
properties.description: Assessed effectiveness: effective, partially-effective, ineffective, not-tested

properties.name: implementationStatus
properties.description: Status: planned, in-progress, implemented, operational

entities.name: RiskOwner
entities.description: A person or organisational role accountable for the management and treatment decisions of assigned information security risks

properties.name: ownerId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the risk owner

properties.name: ownerName
properties.description: Name of the risk owner or role title

properties.name: ownerRole
properties.description: Organisational role of the risk owner

properties.name: authorityLevel
properties.description: Decision authority level: executive, senior-management, management, operational

properties.name: contactEmail
properties.description: Contact email for risk escalation and communication

entities.name: RiskCriteria
entities.description: The criteria and thresholds used to evaluate the significance of risks and determine whether they require treatment

properties.name: criteriaId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the risk criteria set

properties.name: impactScale
properties.description: Definition of impact levels: negligible, minor, moderate, major, catastrophic

properties.name: likelihoodScale
properties.description: Definition of likelihood levels: very-low, low, medium, high, very-high

properties.name: riskAcceptanceThreshold
properties.description: Risk level at or below which risks are accepted without treatment

properties.name: escalationThreshold
properties.description: Risk level at or above which risks must be escalated to senior management

entities.name: ComplianceMapping
entities.description: A mapping that links identified risks and controls to regulatory compliance frameworks within the RCSG scope

properties.name: mappingId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the compliance mapping

properties.name: targetFramework
properties.description: Target compliance framework (e.g., GDPR, MCSB, ISO 27001, NIST)

properties.name: frameworkReference
properties.description: Specific clause, article, or control reference within the framework

properties.name: complianceStatus
properties.description: Status: compliant, partially-compliant, non-compliant, not-assessed

properties.name: evidenceReference
properties.description: Link to compliance evidence or documentation

entities.name: RiskMonitoringPlan
entities.description: An ongoing plan for monitoring, reviewing, and communicating risk status to ensure treatment effectiveness and detect new risks

properties.name: monitoringPlanId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the monitoring plan

properties.name: reviewFrequency
properties.description: How often risks are reviewed: weekly, monthly, quarterly, annually

properties.name: keyRiskIndicators
properties.description: Key risk indicators used for ongoing monitoring

properties.name: reportingChannel
properties.description: Reporting and communication channel for risk updates

properties.name: lastReviewDate
properties.description: Date of the most recent monitoring review

properties.name: planStatus
properties.description: Status: draft, active, suspended, archived

relationships.name: Assesses Context
relationships.description: Links a risk assessment to the organisational context that defines its scope and boundaries

relationships.name: Identifies Risk
relationships.description: Links a risk assessment to the individual risks discovered and evaluated during the assessment

relationships.name: Affects Asset
relationships.description: Links a risk to the information assets that would be adversely impacted if the risk materialises

relationships.name: Exploits Threat
relationships.description: Links a risk to the specific threat that could exploit a vulnerability and cause the unwanted incident

relationships.name: Exploits Vulnerability
relationships.description: Links a risk to the specific vulnerability that a threat may exploit to impact the asset

relationships.name: Owned By Risk Owner
relationships.description: Links a risk to the person or role accountable for its management and treatment decisions

relationships.name: Evaluated Against
relationships.description: Links a risk to the criteria and thresholds used to determine its significance and treatment priority

relationships.name: Treated By
relationships.description: Links a risk to the treatment plan specifying the response strategy to reduce it to acceptable levels

relationships.name: Implements Control
relationships.description: Links a risk treatment to the specific security controls deployed to mitigate the associated risk

relationships.name: Mapped To Compliance
relationships.description: Links a risk to the regulatory and compliance framework mappings within the RCSG scope

relationships.name: Has Monitoring Plan
relationships.description: Links a risk assessment to its ongoing monitoring and review plan for continuous risk oversight

relationships.name: Monitors Risk
relationships.description: Links a monitoring plan to the specific risks under its ongoing review and surveillance scope

relationships.name: Context Defines Criteria
relationships.description: Links the organisational risk context to the risk evaluation criteria it establishes for assessments

relationships.name: Control Maps To Compliance
relationships.description: Links a security control to the compliance framework requirements it satisfies or addresses

businessRules.name: Risk Requires Owner
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk is identified in a RiskAssessment
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one ownedByRiskOwner relationship to a RiskOwner

businessRules.name: Risk Must Reference Asset
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one affectsAsset relationship to an Asset

businessRules.name: Risk Requires Threat And Vulnerability
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST link to at least one Threat via exploitsThreat AND at least one Vulnerability via exploitsVulnerability

businessRules.name: High Risk Must Have Treatment
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk has inherentRiskLevel of high or critical
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one treatedBy relationship to a RiskTreatment with strategy other than accept

businessRules.name: Treatment Must Implement Controls
businessRules.condition: IF a RiskTreatment has treatmentStrategy of mitigate
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one implementsControl relationship to a Control

businessRules.name: Assessment Requires Context
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one assessesContext relationship to a RiskContext

businessRules.name: Assessment Requires Monitoring Plan
businessRules.condition: IF a RiskAssessment status is completed
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a hasMonitoringPlan relationship to a RiskMonitoringPlan with status active

businessRules.name: Risk Evaluation Criteria Required
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST be evaluatedAgainst a RiskCriteria that defines the impact and likelihood scales

businessRules.name: Compliance Mapping For Regulated Risks
businessRules.condition: IF a RiskContext has a regulatoryEnvironment defined
businessRules.action: THEN identified Risks SHOULD have at least one mappedToCompliance relationship to a ComplianceMapping

businessRules.name: Risk Acceptance Requires Approval
businessRules.condition: IF a RiskTreatment has treatmentStrategy of accept
businessRules.action: THEN the associated RiskOwner MUST have authorityLevel of senior-management or executive

bridges.description: RMF RiskContext aligns with the RCSG Framework context for governance scope
ComplianceMapping bridges to GDPR compliance obligations for data protection risks
Controls align with MCSB security benchmark controls

crossOntologyReferences.description: RMF-IS27005 provides ISO 27005 risk management that bridges to GRC-FW for governance scope, GDPR for data protection compliance mapping, and MCSB for security control alignment
